auto_applied.new_value: 16,521 Carrera Coupes; 27,237 Carrera S Coupes worldwide

auto_applied.old_value: ~19,200 worldwide; ~8,870 US combined Coupe and Cabriolet
auto_applied.rationale: Ian Bevis year-by-year arithmetic: Coupe 13+2,150+7,885+4,263+1,315=15,626; Cab 608+4,282+1,209=6,099; total 21,725. Elferspot buyer's guide independently confirms: '15,626 with 6,099 cabrios'. The prior ~19,200 figure appears to be the combined 997.1+997.2 coupe total (15,626+3,301=18,927≈19,200) erroneously attributed to 997.1 alone. US figure refined from ~8,870 to ~8,869 (confirmed: 5,482 coupe + 3,387 cab = 8,869 per multiple US allocation sources).

sources.name: ianbevis.co.uk
sources.note: Year-by-year build table: 997.1 Turbo Coupe by calendar year 2005-2009 sums to 15,626

sources.name: elferspot.com buyer's guide (997 Turbo S)
sources.note: Explicit quote: 'Production numbers for the 997.1 Turbo coupé are at 15,626, with 6,099 cabrios'

sources.name: rennlist.com / Total 911 (via forum)
sources.note: US allocation: 5,482 coupe + 3,387 cab = 8,869 US; conflicting total (19,201) appears to be a coupe-only misread or combined-gen figure

auto_applied.rationale: Ian Bevis year-by-year arithmetic: 1(2005)+44(2006)+2,333(2007)+951(2008)=3,329. Elferspot table independently confirms 3,329. Logic proof: 997.2 GT3=2,256 is confirmed by all sources and exhausts Ian Bevis's 2009-2011 production years (766+1,167+320+3=2,256), leaving 2005-2008 production (3,329) entirely for the 997.1 GT3. The prior ~2,370 figure (≈2,378 from Total 911/porscheknowledge) is inconsistent with this arithmetic—those sources appear to have excluded the MY2008 production run of 951 units.

conflicting_sources.note: Excludes 2008 production run; likely Mk1-only figure

conflicting_sources.name: Total 911 magazine (via Rennlist forum)
conflicting_sources.note: Same exclusion as porscheknowledge; both contradict Ian Bevis arithmetic

auto_applied.rationale: Ian Bevis year-by-year arithmetic: 2(2005)+9(2006)+1,095(2007)+803(2008)+1(2009)=1,910. Elferspot confirms 1,909 (1-unit rounding vs Ian Bevis). GT3RS registry independently shows 1,909 worldwide with 413 US. US figure tightened from ~410 to ~413 per registry data. Minor numerical precision improvement from an already-correct approximate figure.
Three independent sources agree on 1,242. Tilde removed.

sources.name: Search result synthesis
sources.note: 1,242 cited consistently across GT2/GT3 production discussions

auto_applied.rationale: Exact figures already confirmed in variant-level production fields from prior audit. Summary field updated to match. Approximations and trailing '(approx.)' removed.

Ian Bevis year-by-year arithmetic for 997.2 GT3 RS 3.8: 1,223(2010)+295(2010.2R)+100(2011)=1,618≈1,619. Elferspot confirms 1,619. nweuro.com confirms NA: 541 US + 71 Canada = 612. The prior ~2,000 figure appears to have conflated the 997.2 GT3 RS 3.8L with a combined-RS total or was based on an expected/announced production target rather than actual builds. NOTE: The separate GT3 RS 4.0 (613 built per Ian Bevis, vs '600 worldwide cap' in file) is tracked as a distinct variant and its count is not merged here.
Elferspot buyer's guide provides exact coupe/cab breakdown (3,095 + 2,055 = 5,150). Tilde removed from worldwide total; coupe/cab split added for data consistency with other variant entries.
